page: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is a general-purpose server instance powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core running at 3.4 GHz, supported by 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The CPU cache architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. This instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local ephemeral storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachments.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The multi-core stress-ng score of 132422.15 and Geekbench multi-core score of 23434.0 both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7272.24 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 203.11 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, with a PassMark database score of 38123.37, and static web serving throughput of 93.05 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

The D64ps_v6 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads due to its 64 dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. This architecture ensures predictable execution for multi-threaded tasks but presents a tradeoff in single-threaded execution speeds and workloads requiring local ephemeral storage. It is highly suited for scale-out workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database management, and medium-sized language model inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is a general-purpose server instance powered by a 64-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Lacking local storage and integrated GPUs, the instance relies on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core capabilities, with outstanding memory bandwidth up to 7272.24 GB/sec cached read and exceptional multi-threaded performance in software compilation and database operations. However, single-core performance remains average. The dedicated physical cores deliver highly predictable performance, making this instance cost-efficient for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, database management, multi-threaded applications, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and web workloads.

tagline: A 64-core ARM64 instance delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and massive memory bandwidth for high-throughput database and web workloads.

256.0 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
